query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Indigent', 'Lacking food, clothing, etc because of poverty', 'The reviewer described the tone as indigent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indigestible', 'Difficult or impossible to digest', 'Critics argued that the plan might indigestible the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indigestion', 'A common medical condition often caused by eating quickly', 'Her indigestion became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indignant', 'Feeling or showing anger at unfair treatment', 'Scholars noted a striking indignant between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indignity', 'An injury to a person''s dignity', 'Her indignity became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indiscernible', 'Not discernible, that cannot be seen or perceived clearly', 'Scholars noted a striking indiscernible between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indiscreet', 'Not discreet, lacking prudence or circumspection', 'Scholars noted a striking indiscreet between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indiscriminate', 'Not discriminating, lacking in care, judgment, etc', 'Over time, the community learned to indiscriminate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indispensable', 'Absolutely necessary, essential, or requisite', 'The passage presents a indispensable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indistinct', 'Not clearly defined or distinguishable', 'The reviewer described the tone as indistinct, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indivertible', 'Not to be diverted or turned aside', 'Critics argued that the plan might indivertible the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Individualistic', 'Independent and self-reliant; not conforming to a group', 'Over time, the community learned to individualistic its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indoctrinate', 'To teach a person or group to accept a set of beliefs uncritically', 'Over time, the community learned to indoctrinate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indolence', 'Habitual laziness or sloth', 'Her indolence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indolent', 'Wanting to avoid activity or exertion; lazy', 'The author hoped the new policy would indolent tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indomitable', 'Impossible to subdue or defeat', 'Over time, the community learned to indomitable its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indubitable', 'Clearly true, providing no possibility of doubt', 'What seemed indubitable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Induce', 'To bring about or cause something to happen', '“The doctor used medication to induce sleep.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Induct', 'To formally or ceremoniously install in an office, position', 'The author hoped the new policy would induct tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inductee', 'A person who is inducted into an organization', 'Her inductee became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indulge', '(often followed by "in"): to yield to a temptation or desire', 'Critics argued that the plan might indulge the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Indulgently', 'In a manner that is excessively lenient or permissive', 'She spoke indulgently, leaving little doubt about her position.', 'adverb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Industrious', 'Working energetically and devotedly, hard-working', 'What seemed industrious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inebriate', 'To cause to be drunk', 'The author hoped the new policy would inebriate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inebriation', 'The state or characteristic of drunkenness', 'The essay highlights the inebriation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inedible', 'Unfit to be eaten', 'Scholars noted a striking inedible between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ineffable', 'Too great or extreme to be expressed in words', '“The beauty of the sunset was ineffable.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ineffectual', 'Not producing the desired effect', '“The negotiations were ineffectual, leading to a continued stalemate.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inefficacious', 'Not producing the desired effect', 'What seemed inefficacious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inefficient', 'Unable to achieve the desired result economically', 'The reviewer described the tone as inefficient, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ineligible', 'Not eligible,  not permitted or suitable', 'Her ineligible became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ineluctable', 'Inevitable, unable to be avoided or escaped', 'Over time, the community learned to ineluctable its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inept', 'Having or showing no skill; clumsy', 'What seemed inept at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inert', 'Unable to move or act, inanimate', 'Critics argued that the plan might inert the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inertia', 'The property of a body that resists any change to its motion', 'Critics argued that the plan might inertia the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inestimable', 'Not able to be estimated as because of great scale or degree', 'Critics argued that the plan might inestimable the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inevitable', 'Certain to happen; unavoidable', '“With the rising costs, price increases seemed inevitable.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inexcusable', 'Not excusable', 'What seemed inexcusable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inexhaustible', 'Impossible to exhaust, unlimited', 'Critics argued that the plan might inexhaustible the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inexorable', 'Impossible to stop or prevent', '“The passage of time is inexorable.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inexpedient', 'Not tending to the end desired, inadvisable, unfit, improper', 'The author hoped the new policy would inexpedient tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inexpensive', 'Low in price', 'The passage presents a inexpensive portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inexperience', 'A lack of experience', 'Her inexperience became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inexplicable', 'Impossible to explain, not easily accounted for', 'Over time, the community learned to inexplicable its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inexpressible', 'Unable to be expressed', 'The author hoped the new policy would inexpressible tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inextensible', 'Not capable of being extended', 'The reviewer described the tone as inextensible, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inextricable', 'Impossible to untie or disentangle', 'Over time, the community learned to inextricable its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infallible', 'Incapable of making mistakes or being wrong', 'Her infallible became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infamous', 'Having a bad reputation, of bad report, notoriously vile', 'The passage presents a infamous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Infamy', 'Extremely bad reputation, public reproach', 'The passage presents a infamy portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
